servinfo.title: Front Strut Assembly Removal and Installation

attention3.ptxt: Pulling steering knuckle outward with drive shaft fixed to steering knuckle could cause drive shaft to be removed from transaxle or intermediate shaft.
Do not pull steering knuckle outward.
If drive shaft is removed, be sure to replace drive shaft circlip with new one.

When servicing strut assembly components, loosen strut support upper nut a little before removing strut assembly. This will facilitate service work. Note that the nut must not be removed at this point.

s1.ptxt: Hoist vehicle, allowing front suspension to be free of vehicle weight.
Remove stabilizer joint nut while holding stud using hexagon wrench (2) and then disconnect stabilizer upper joint (1) from strut (3).
Remove brake hose mounting bolt (1), to detach brake hose (2), front wheel speed sensor harness (3) and clip (7) from front strut (4) as shown in figure.
Remove strut bracket nuts (5) and bolts (6).
Remove strut support upper nut (1) and rebound stopper (2), then remove front strut assembly from vehicle.

attention3.ptxt: Hold strut assembly by hand so that it will not fall off.

servinfosub.title: Installation

ptxt.intxrefpoff: Front Strut Assembly Disassembly and Reassembly

attention4.ptxt: The strut bracket nut is pre-coated with friction stabilizer. If the nut is reused, it may work loose.
Never reuse the strut bracket nut.

servinfosub.ptxt: Reverse removal procedure, noting the following points.

item.ptxt: Insert strut bracket bolts (1) in the direction shown in figure.
Tighten all fasteners to specified torque.
Do not twist brake hose and front wheel speed sensor harness when installing them.
Install front wheel speed sensor harness by fitting its grommet (3) on hook of bracket (2).
Lower hoist with vehicle in unloaded condition, then tighten strut support upper nut to specified torque.
After completing installation, check front wheel alignment.
